data.title: Star Group, L.P. (NYSE: SGU) to release Q3 results on Aug 6, 2025, to host webcast/conference call on Aug 7, 2025 at 11 AM ET.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, in Stamford, Connecticut, Star Group, L.P. (NYSE: SGU), a prominent home energy distributor and services provider, shared plans to unveil its fiscal 2025 third-quarter results on August 6, 2025, after trading hours. The company's management team will conduct a webcast and conference call at 11:00 a.m. Eastern Time on August 7, 2025, to analyze the financial performance for the three and nine months concluding on June 30, 2025.

data.title: Ferrari N.V. to continue €2 billion share repurchase program with an eighth tranche of up to €360 million starting on August 22, 2025.
data.description: Ferrari N.V. intends to extend its ongoing share buyback program, initially set at around Euro 2 billion as announced in 2022, with an eighth tranche of up to Euro 360 million. The buyback program is scheduled to commence on August 22, 2025, and conclude by December 18, 2025. This latest phase marks a continuation of the multi-year initiative outlined in the 2022 Capital Markets Day, demonstrating Ferrari's commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

data.title: Ex-OpenSea manager's conviction overturned in historic crypto insider trading case involving wire fraud and money laundering in 2023.
data.description: In 2023, Nathanial Chastain, a former product manager at OpenSea, was found guilty of wire fraud and money laundering. He had illegally used insider information on NFT listings to make a profit.

data.title: Echogen & Westinghouse partner for Europe's first grid-scale pumped thermal energy storage system in Slovakia.
data.description: An agreement has been signed between Westinghouse Electric Company, a partner of Echogen Power Systems, and Vodohospodárska Výstavba (VVB) to collaborate on Europe's inaugural grid-scale pumped storage project. This partnership marks a significant milestone in the region's energy sector. The agreement was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) and aims to advance the development of the project. This strategic move is set to propel the energy industry forward, with the project anticipated to significantly enhance the grid's storage capacity by embedding innovative solutions. The collaboration entails careful planning and implementation, marking a crucial step towards achieving sustainable energy goals. Europe's energy landscape is poised to experience a transformative shift with this pioneering endeavor.

data.title: Agricruit, a fast-growing recruiting firm in agriculture, acquired by Iris Nevins, a software engineering leader. Vikings Mergers & Acquisitions facilitated the deal. TAMPA, Fla. July 31, 2025.
data.description: In a transaction advised by Viking Mergers & Acquisitions, on July 31, 2025, Agricruit, a rapidly expanding recruitment agency in the agricultural sector, was purchased by Iris Nevins. Nevins, a prominent figure in software engineering and entrepreneurship, led the acquisition of Agricruit, which is known for its substantial presence in the industry. This acquisition marks a significant move in the agricultural recruitment sector, especially as Agricruit has been recognized as one of the fastest-growing firms in the nation. The deal signifies a noteworthy development in the career placement market, with specific emphasis on the agricultural field.

data.title: Boise Cascade faces strike as Teamsters Local 190 demand fair pay and healthcare benefits in Billings, Mont.
data.description: Employees at Boise Cascade in Billings, Montana have initiated a strike as the company continues to delay contract negotiations. Teamsters Local 190 members are demanding better pay raises and enhanced healthcare benefits. The strike officially began on July 31, 2025. This walkout involves a significant number of workers and has garnered attention for its focus on fair compensation and improved healthcare provisions.
Figma $FIG shares expected to open for trading today at $90-$95, well over the $33 IPO price.

data.title: Biogen's Q2 2025 earnings per share were $5.47, up 4%, with sales of $2.65 billion, up 7%, beating analyst expectations. Multiple sclerosis revenue at $1.1 billion, down 4%. Rare disease revenue grew 2% to $543 million. Spinraza's revenue decreased to $392.7 million. Acquisition of Skyclarys brought in $130.3 million in revenue.
data.description: Biogen Inc. (NASDAQ: BIIB) announced its Q2 2025 financial results, with adjusted earnings per share at $5.47, marking a 4% rise from the previous year and surpassing analyst expectations of $4.06. The company's sales also saw an increase, reaching $2.65 billion, up 7% year over year on a reported basis and 8% on a constant currency basis, beating the consensus of $2.32 billion. Revenue generated from multiple sclerosis amounted to $1.1 billion, showing a 4% decrease year over year (constant currency down by 4%), with sales of the drug Tysabri dropping to $454.6 million from $462.2 million. Meanwhile, rare disease revenue experienced a 2% rise year over year (3% on constant currency) to $543 million. Spinraza's revenue declined to $392.7 million from $429.1 million in the same period the previous year. Additionally, revenues from Skyclarys, acquired through the Reata acquisition, increased to $130.3 million compared to $100 million previously. This positive outcome was accompanied by news regarding a partnered drug with Ionis showing benefits for children with SMA who had prior treatment with Novartis' Zolgensma.

data.title: Universal Music Group N.V. reports €2,980M revenue in Q2 2025, up 1.6% YoY. Recorded Music & Music Publishing drive growth.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, the company reported revenue of €2,980 million, marking a 1.6% year-over-year increase. In constant currency terms, this growth was 4.5%. The growth was primarily fueled by the Recorded Music and Music Publishing segments. Specifically, Recorded Music revenue saw a 1.1% year-over-year increase, or 3.9% in constant currency. The Music Publishing segment also contributed to the overall growth.

data.title: Urban Engineers, Inc. appoints James Bilella, PE, and Christopher Bobrowski, CPA, as interim CEO and CFO respectively. 65th Anniversary celebration scheduled.
data.description: James Bilella, a professional engineer, and Christopher Bobrowski, a certified public accountant, are set to lead the firm Urban Engineers, Inc. as it marks its 65th anniversary. The Philadelphia-based company focuses on various disciplines including planning, engineering design, and environmental services. This news was announced on July 31, 2025, and signifies a new chapter for Urban as it reaches this milestone.

data.title: VINCI releases 2025 half-year financial report, submitted to French regulator. Report available in English and French online.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, Nanterre-based company VINCI released its 2025 half-year financial report and submitted it to the Autorité des marchés financiers for regulatory approval. The report can be accessed in both English and French on VINCI's official website (www.vinci.com) under the Investors section. VINCI, a global leader in concessions and energy solutions, offers innovative services in various sectors. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Inventor creates Trailer Crane to load plastic wrap onto cotton stripper, resolving issue for farmers.
data.description: In Levelland, Texas, an inventor created the TRAILER CRANE FOR PLASTIC WRAP ROLL FOR COTTON STRIPPER to assist farmers with loading heavy plastic wrap rolls. The invention eliminates the need for two people to handle the task, streamlining the process. The device is expected to improve efficiency and safety for farmers working with cotton strippers. The invention was introduced on July 31, 2025, in Pittsburgh.

data.title: Teleperformance's H1 2025 revenue: €5,116M, up +1.5%; Core Services revenue growth +2.9%; Q2 2025 revenue growth +3.5%.
data.description: In the first half of 2025, Teleperformance reported a group revenue of €5,116 million, showing a 1.5% increase like-for-like. This growth was driven by a boost in Core Services revenue, with a growth of 2.9% in the first half. Revenue growth further accelerated in the second quarter of 2025 to 3.5% like-for-like compared to 2.3% in the first quarter of 2025. Specifically, in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific, revenue surged by 5.7% like-for-like in Q2, compared to 3.8% in Q1. This growth was attributed to enhanced client retention and new business opportunities. Additionally, Teleperformance experienced a ramp-up in new value streams linked to bac.

data.title: Euronext's Q2 2025 results show revenue reaching €465.8M, up 12.8%, with adjusted EBITDA at €297.3M (+15.8%). Company expands in Nordics, UK. Acquisition offer for ATHEX shares made. Margin at 63.8%. Net debt at 1.8x EBITDA. 5 consecutive quarters of double-digit growth.
data.description: Euronext, the European capital market infrastructure, reported Q2 2025 revenue and income of €465.8 million, up by 12.8% from the previous year. Non-volume-related revenue constituted 58% of total revenue and helped exceed operational expenses by 161%. Adjusted EBITDA was €297.3 million (+15.8%), net income at €183.8 million (+29.7%), and EPS at €1.81 (+32.1%). Euronext aimed to acquire ATHEX, valuing it at approximately €412.8 million. It also launched initiatives to enhance its clearing services and expand its European presence. Additionally, it issued convertible bonds and acquired Admincontrol for Nordics expansion.
Orange released their financial report for 2025. Significant numbers disclosed.
